titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to Records of Ecumenical Work for Coordination and Documentation of Missions and World Christianity
Records of Ecumenical Work for Coordination and Documentation of Missions and World Christianity
titlestmt.author: Martha Lund Smalley

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday October 14, 2024 at 22:00

did.unittitle: Records of Ecumenical Work for Coordination and Documentation of Missions and World Christianity

did.unittitle: International Association for the Mission Studies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See Record Group 199: International Association for Mission Studies Records

did.unittitle: Madras meeting: "Study booklets on Hangchow topics"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Location of meeting was changed from Hangchow to Madras [now Chennai] due to war conditions in China. The meeting was held in Tambaram, a section of Chennai.

Madras meeting: articles about meeting in journals and newspapers

Willingen meeting: preparatory material - territorial surveys
Willingen meeting: reports of Groups I - V, comments
Willingen meeting: miscellaneous reports, statements, reflections

Committee on the Christian Approach to the Jews
Commission on the Nature and Meaning of the Church
Yale-Edinburgh Group on World Christianity and the History of Missions

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Andrew Walls (University of Edinburgh) and Lamin Sanneh (Yale University) founded the Yale Edinburgh group in 1992. The Group's conferences and LISTSERV facilitate discussion and exchange of information about the development of world Christianity and historical aspects of mission, with special emphasis on the sources for documentation. Its annual conferences have been hosted alternately by the Centre for the Study of World Christianity at the University of Edinburgh and the Yale Divinity School and co-sponsored by the Overseas Ministries Study Center.

did.unittitle: Statement regarding first conferences; background paper by Lamin Sanneh "From Christendom to World Christianity"
Article by Prof. Dana L. Robert: "Naming 'World Christianity': Historical and Personal Perspectives on the Yale-Edinburgh Conference in World Christianity and Mission History"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Originally delivered as the keynote address at the 2019 Yale-Edinburgh Group conference, published in the International Bulletin of Mission Research , 2019.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The theme statements, or call for papers, of the conferences were prepared by the conveners of the Yale-Edinburgh Group - Andrew Walls and Lamin Sanneh for many years, followed by other representatives from Yale Divinity School and the University of Edinburgh Centre for the Study of World Christianity. Full conference programs and theme statements are not available for all years. Selected papers presented at the conferences are available in digital format at the Yale Divinity Library. Contact the Special Collections Librarian for more information.

did.unittitle: "From Christendom to World Christianity," at YDS (March) and "The Missionary Movement: Inter-continental Connexions" at Edinburgh (September)
"The Churches and the Missionary Movement" at Edinburgh

scopecontent.p: No records available.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See the Divinity Library's cataloged collection for membership information, newsletters and conference reports of the British and Irish Association for Mission Studies (BIAMS).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The British Student Volunteer Movement was established in April, 1892. Additional documentation is available in the Divinity Library's cataloged collection.

scopecontent.p: Includes papers presented at the biennial meetings of the Association by notable missions scholars such as Joseph Kitagawa, Kenneth Cragg, Miner Searle Bates, Creighton Lacy, Donald McGavran.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Conferences included here were not sponsored by a particular mission agency or organization. They are primarily ecumenical in nature, gathering together on one-time or repeated occasions missionaries or people with an interest in missions. Search the Divinity Library's online catalog for additional instances of such conferences, such as the China Centenary Missionary Conference held in Shanghai in 1907.

did.unittitle: World Missionary Conference, Edinburgh 1910 and Continuation Committees
Commission I: "Carrying the Gospel to all the World" - questionnaire replies from China missionaries: A - U

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: These documents, arranged alphabetically by name of missionary, describe their mission work in China.

Commission IV: "The Missionary Message in Relation to Non-Christian Religions - questionnaire replies from missionaries
Minutes and reports for 1912 meeting at Lake Mohonk
Minutes and reports for 1913 meeting at The Hague
China: Photograph of attendees at South China Regional Continuation Conference

Rhodesia / Southern Rhodesia Missionary Conference proceedings (1913-1922); Conference of Missionaries of Southern Rhodesia with John R. Mott (1934)
General Missionary Conference of South Africa - description, correspondence
Ecumenical Dialogue of Third World Theologians, at Dar es Salaam, Tanzania; Pan African Conference of Third World Theologians at Accra, Ghana
General Conference of the Protestant Missionaries of China, held at Shanghai 1877, 1890

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See the Divinity Library's cataloged collection for records of these conferences, as well as of the China Centenary Missionary Conference of 1907.

did.unittitle: First General Conference of the Christian Churches of West China, held at Union University in Chengtu, Szechwan [Chengdu, Sichuan] - program and report

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See the Divinity Library's cataloged collection for records related to additional conferences in India, such as the South India Missionary Conference, General Missionary Conference, and General Conference on Missions to Muslims.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Additional reports of this conference are available in the Divinity Library's cataloged collection.

did.unittitle: General Conference of Christian Workers, Egypt, the Sudan, Abyssinia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This conference, chaired by John R. Mott, was one of various conferences sponsored by the International Missionary Council. Archives of the International Missionary Council, including documentation of its Research and Study Program, are available on microfiche at the Divinity Library.

did.unittitle: Ecumenical Conference on Foreign Missions, held at New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: See the Divinity Library's cataloged collection for additional documentation.

did.unittitle: Connecticut Valley Student Missionary Conference, held at Wesleyan University, CT

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For extensive additional documentation of student missionary conferences, see Record Group 42 - Student Volunteer Movement for Foreign Missions records, Record Group 46 - World Student Christian Federation records, and Record Group 57 - Student Christian Movement in New England records

did.unittitle: Ecumenical Student Conference on the Christian World Mission, held at Athens, Ohio

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For extensive additional documentation of student missionary conferences, see Record Group 42 - Student Volunteer Movement for Foreign Missions records, Record Group 46 - World Student Christian Federation records, Record Group 57 - Student Christian Movement in New England records, and Records Group 257 - National Student Christian Federation Records.

did.unittitle: Study Conference: World Students and the Christian Church, held at Princeton, NJ
Conference on the Christian Community in the Third World, held at Dayton, OH
Hosted or co-sponsored by the Yale Divinity School

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The aim of the Ecumenical Leaders Index Project, an endeavor funded by the Episcopal Church Foundation, was to create a database of information regarding individuals, issues, and events connected with the ecumenical movement during the time period 1935-1960. Project staff surveyed all relevant manuscript and archival collections at the Yale Divinity Library to discover correspondence and papers of the individuals selected for inclusion in the Index. Database entry printouts provide biographical information for the ecumenical leaders and listings of available archival resources related to them.

did.unittitle: Project description; list of individuals researched for database

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Mission Studies Resource Development Project began in 1993 and over the next 18 months investigated the international base of research resources for mission studies and the study of world Christianity. It was sponsored by a grant from The Pew Charitable Trusts. Stephen L. Peterson, Director of the Trinity College Library, was the project director. Project staff included Jonathan J. Bonk, Project Associate (Providence College and Seminary, Otterburne, Manitoba, Canada) and Joan Duffy, Research Assistant (Yale Divinity Library).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains documentation related to the investigations undertaken by the Mission Studies Resource Development Project. The Project employed three primary methods to garner information: mailed questionnaires, site visits, and consultations. Different questionnaires were constructed to be used with different populations: mission agencies, overseas church bodies, libraries, archives, and individuals.

did.unittitle: Correspondence - gathering information re. parties to be surveyed
Correspondence - Peterson site visits to Ghana and India
Correspondence - individuals interested in results of surveys
Survey tools - final questionnaires and drafts
Cumulative statistics for archival repository questionnaires
Completed questionnaires from libraries and educational institutions ("Factories")
Completed questionnaires from church bodies and mission agencies ("Mines")
Completed questionnaires from mission studies scholars ("Personal")
Final Project report - "Mission Studies Resources for the Future" by Stephen L. Peterson
Report on Ghana site visit by Jon Bonk
European Mission Resource Centers report by John Roxborogh
Articles related to mission study resources in International Bulletin of Missionary Research

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: NAMP was an academic initiative funded by the Pew Charitable Trusts that began in 1996. It was directed by Dr. Brian Stanley, based at the University of Cambridge. A selection of papers prepared for the North Atlantic Missiology Project are available on the Yale Divinity Library server. Contact the Special Collections Librarian for more information.
